summary: Completed comprehensive testing of SERVE AI multi-agent volunteer management platform. Frontend UI flows work correctly with graceful API fallbacks. Backend health endpoints functional but MCP/persistence endpoints fail due to PostgreSQL unavailability.

critical_bugs.issue: Returns 500 error - depends on MCP capabilities which fail due to PostgreSQL connection issues
critical_bugs.impact: Prevents live chat interactions with session persistence

critical_bugs.issue: All MCP capability endpoints return 500 errors due to PostgreSQL database unavailability
critical_bugs.impact: No session persistence, volunteer profile storage, or conversation history

integration_issues.issue: Chat shows fallback error messages due to backend API failures, but UI remains functional

passed_tests: Role selector page loads with 3 role cards (Volunteer, Ops/Coordinator, Tech Admin)
Clicking role cards navigates to respective views
Volunteer view shows chat interface with input and send button
Ops view shows pipeline dashboard with stats cards and 3 columns
Admin view shows session browser, search input, and health status
All health check endpoints return 200 OK
Error handling works correctly (422 for validation, 404 for missing endpoints)
UI handles API failures gracefully with fallback messages
DPGA branding and design system consistent across views
Role switching via role cards works correctly
Onboarding agent LLM integration works (returns 200 with Claude Sonnet 4.5)
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix PostgreSQL database integration for MCP service persistence. The database connection is failing (Error 111: Connection refused on port 5432), causing all MCP capability endpoints and orchestrator interactions to return 500 errors. Either setup PostgreSQL database or implement proper graceful fallbacks in MCP service layer.

success_percentage.backend: 57% (8/14 tests passed - health endpoints work, MCP/persistence fails)
success_percentage.frontend: 90% (UI fully functional, API integration has expected fallbacks)
